title: OEE Metrics Collection
spl:
index=ot sourcetype="opcua:tag" tag=oee
| bin _time span=1h
| stats avg(availability_pct) as A, avg(performance_pct) as P, avg(quality_pct) as Q by line_id, _time
| eval oee=round((A*P*Q)/10000,2)
| where oee < 0.75
| sort _time
description: Availability √ó Performance √ó Quality from SCADA/MES tags ‚Äî executive and plant KPIs.
value: Availability √ó Performance √ó Quality from SCADA/MES tags ‚Äî executive and plant KPIs.
implementation: Align tag naming per ISA-95; validate against manual OEE samples monthly.
visualization: Line chart (OEE trend), Gauge (current OEE), Bar chart (loss buckets).
knownFalsePositives: Similar activity during production startups, control downloads, and approved remote maintenance‚Äîtriage with the SCADA HMI and change log.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Historian, OPC-UA, MES.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="opcua:tag"`, `sourcetype="historian:sample"`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Align tag naming per ISA-95; validate against manual OEE samples monthly.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot sourcetype="opcua:tag" tag=oee
| bin _time span=1h
| stats avg(availability_pct) as A, avg(performance_pct) as P, avg(quality_pct) as Q by line_id, _time
| eval oee=round((A*P*Q)/10000,2)
| where oee < 0.75
| sort _time
```

Understanding this SPL

**OEE Metrics Collection** ‚Äî Availability √ó Performance √ó Quality from SCADA/MES tags ‚Äî executive and plant KPIs.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="opcua:tag"`, `sourcetype="historian:sample"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Historian, OPC-UA, MES. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot; **sourcetype**: opcua:tag. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=ot, sourcetype="opcua:tag", tags. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by line_id, _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **oee** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where oee < 0.75` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Run the search when the process is in a known state. Compare key fields and alarm states to the SCADA or DCS HMI and, where relevant, the engineering tool for the PLC. Confirm sourcetypes and field extractions match the vendor feed.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (OEE trend), Gauge (current OEE), Bar chart (loss buckets).
grandmaExplanation: We watch your plant control systems and field data. We help you know if something is off with this area: oee metrics collection before harm, damage, or wasted effort piles up.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable (no CIM or Operational_Telemetry data model in this SPL).
